# docs(agentos): author the model-provider guide — local vs remote (the local-models achievement)

Part of #14310 · follow the content rubric pinned on #14310. Refs #14310.

**Problem:** running the Agent OS on **local models** (gemma4 / qwen3-embedding — privacy, zero API cost, on-prem / air-gap; the ~1,000-PR achievement) vs **remote** (Gemini Flash 2.5 / gemini-embed-001) has no first-class guide; the choice is scattered.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] New guide on the model-provider axis (chat + embedding): local (Ollama / llama.cpp — privacy / cost / on-prem) vs remote (Gemini); why-care (local was the hard part + the enterprise unlock).
- [ ] How to choose + configure (the `aiConfig` leaves / env vars); folds / links `LlamaCppProfile.md`.
- [ ] Registered in `tree.json`.

**Files:** new `learn/agentos/ModelProviders.md` (name TBD) + `learn/tree.json` (+ fold `cloud-deployment/LlamaCppProfile.md`).

## Grounding Feed from #14333 — Model-provider source boundaries

This is source-grounding for the eventual writing pass, not a substitute for the #14323 author doing the required memory-mine/use-tools/V-B-A before drafting.

#14333 checked the provider row and concluded this ticket is the correct owner. No extra #14310 sub is needed for provider helper internals.

### Verified source shape

- Selector axes are distinct: `NEO_MODEL_PROVIDER` / `chatProvider`, legacy `modelProvider`, `NEO_GRAPH_PROVIDER`, and `NEO_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER` live in `ai/config.template.mjs:120-203`.
- Local model limits are role-based, not provider-based: `localModels.chat` vs `localModels.embedding` in `ai/config.template.mjs:205-318`.
- Chat/synthesis routes through `ai/provider/buildChatModel.mjs:65-226` for `gemini`, `openAiCompatible`, and `ollama`; local chat is queued, Gemini is not.
- OpenAI-compatible chat uses `/v1/chat/completions` (`ai/provider/OpenAiCompatible.mjs:352-359`).
- OpenAI-compatible embeddings are not on the provider class; `TextEmbeddingService` owns `/v1/embeddings` (`ai/services/memory-core/TextEmbeddingService.mjs:428-545`) and dispatches single/batch embeddings across `openAiCompatible`, `ollama`, and `gemini` (`:680-809`).
- Native Ollama embeddings use `/api/embed` (`ai/provider/Ollama.mjs:467-540`).
- Graph generation has its own provider axis and supports `ollama` + `openAiCompatible` (`ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.mjs:4-104`).
- KB ask synthesis uses a dedicated `askSynthesis` provider block through `buildChatModel`, so the interactive ask path can differ from bulk chat/summarization (`ai/services/knowledge-base/SearchService.mjs:67-109`).
- KB health treats local embedding providers as local and only requires `GEMINI_API_KEY` for remote `gemini` (`ai/services/knowledge-base/HealthService.mjs:274-303`).
- `LlamaCppProfile.md` correctly uses the existing `openAiCompatible` selector and explicitly rejects documenting a fake `llamaCpp` provider key.

### Guide implication

The guide should tell the **local vs remote model-mode story** as a reader-facing choice, while keeping it orthogonal to **LOCAL vs CLOUD Agent OS** topology. It should explain why the local-model achievement matters, how chat/embedding/graph/ask routing differs, and where operators verify readiness. It should link/fold reference surfaces (`LlamaCppProfile.md`, deployment config, healthcheck output) instead of inlining a helper/env catalog.
